source_paper_title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models Are Optimally Adaptive to Unknown Low Dimensionality
context:
Page 16, Section 6 (Discussion)

"To begin with, it remains unclear how the DDPM sampler adapts to unknown low dimensionality when other metrics (e.g.\, the total-variation distance or the Wasserstein distance) are used to measure distributional discrepancy."
title: Adaptivity of DDPM to Unknown Low Dimensionality Under Non-KL Metrics
latex:
Given an ambient dimension \(d\in\mathbb{N}\), let \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) be a probability distribution on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) with support \(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}}\subseteq \mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying the intrinsic-dimension condition: there exist universal constants \(C_0,C_{\mathrm{cover}}>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0:=k^{-C_0}\) such that
\[
\log N_{\mathrm{cover}}(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}},\|\cdot\|_2,\varepsilon_0)\le C_{\mathrm{cover}}\,k\log\frac{1}{\varepsilon_0},
\]
where \(N_{\mathrm{cover}}(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}},\|\cdot\|_2,\varepsilon_0)\) is the minimal number of Euclidean balls of radius \(\varepsilon_0\) needed to cover \(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}}\). Consider the forward Ornstein--Uhlenbeck diffusion \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) in \(\mathbb{R}^d\):
\[
\mathrm{d}X_t=-X_t\,\mathrm{d}t+\sqrt{2}\,\mathrm{d}W_t,\qquad X_0\sim p_{\mathrm{data}},
\]
and the standard DDPM sampler \((Y_{t_n})_{n=0}^{N}\) defined on a discretization \(0=t_0<t_1<\cdots<t_N=T-\delta\) with coefficients \(\alpha_n=e^{-2(t_{n+1}-t_n)}\) and perfect score access \(s_t(x)=\nabla\log q_t(x)\), where \(q_t\) is the law of \(X_t\). Let \(p_{\mathrm{out}}\) denote the law of the DDPM output \(Y_{t_N}\), and let \(q_{\delta}\) denote the law of \(X_{\delta}\) (i.e., the data distribution convolved with Gaussian noise corresponding to time \(\delta\)).

Fix a distributional discrepancy metric \(\mathcal{D}(\cdot,\cdot)\) that is not KL divergence, e.g. total variation distance \(\mathrm{TV}(\cdot,\cdot)\) or Wasserstein distance \(W_p(\cdot,\cdot)\) for some \(p\ge 1\).

Determine whether there exist assumptions on \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) comparable in strength to the intrinsic-dimension and bounded-support assumptions above, and a discretization schedule \(\{t_n\}\), under which the DDPM sampler achieves an \(\varepsilon\)-accuracy guarantee
\[
\mathcal{D}(q_{\delta},p_{\mathrm{out}})\le \varepsilon
\]
with an iteration complexity bound \(N\) scaling nearly linearly in the intrinsic dimension \(k\) (up to logarithmic factors) rather than in the ambient dimension \(d\).
open_problem_quote: "To begin with, it remains unclear how the DDPM sampler adapts to unknown low dimensionality when other metrics (e.g.\, the total-variation distance or the Wasserstein distance) are used to measure distributional discrepancy."
subject_classification: Stochastic Processes / Sampling and Monte Carlo (Diffusion Models)
